audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for garden arrest, ego eimi arrest scene, Peter sword, Annas/Caiaphas, Peter denial, Pilate, kingdom not from this world, truth, Barabbas.

verses.restored: Having said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples across the winter-flowing Kidron, where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.
Judas, the one handing him over, also knew the place, because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
Therefore Judas, taking the cohort and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, comes there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.
Jesus therefore, knowing all the things coming upon him, went out and says to them, Whom do you seek?
They answered him, Jesus the Nazarene. He says to them, I am. Judas, the one handing him over, also stood with them.
When therefore he said to them, I am, they went back and fell to the ground.
Again therefore he questioned them, Whom do you seek? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
Jesus answered, I told you that I am. If therefore you seek me, let these go away,
so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: Of those whom you have given me, I lost not one.
Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.
Jesus therefore said to Peter, Put the sword into the sheath. The cup that the Father has given me, shall I not drink it?
So the cohort, commander, and officers of the Judeans seized Jesus and bound him.
They led him first to Annas, for he was father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
Caiaphas was the one who had counseled the Judeans that it was advantageous for one human to die for the people.
Simon Peter was following Jesus, and another disciple. That disciple was known to the high priest and entered with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.
But Peter stood at the door outside. The other disciple, the one known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the doorkeeper and brought Peter in.
The servant girl, the doorkeeper, says to Peter, Are you not also from this human's disciples? He says, I am not.
The servants and officers had made a charcoal fire, because it was cold, and were standing and warming themselves. Peter also was with them, standing and warming himself.
The high priest therefore questioned Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.
Jesus answered him, I have spoken openly to the world; I always taught in synagogue and in the temple, where all the Judeans come together, and in hiddenness I spoke nothing.
Why do you question me? Question those who have heard what I spoke to them; look, these know what I said.
When he said these things, one of the officers standing by gave Jesus a strike, saying, Is this how you answer the high priest?
Jesus answered him, If I spoke harmfully, bear witness concerning the harm; but if beautifully/well, why do you strike me?
Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.
Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said to him, Are you not also from his disciples? He denied and said, I am not.
One of the servants of the high priest, being a relative of the one whose ear Peter cut off, says, Did I not see you in the garden with him?
Again therefore Peter denied, and immediately a rooster sounded.
Then they lead Jesus from Caiaphas to the praetorium. It was early. They themselves did not enter the praetorium, so that they might not be defiled but might eat the Passover.
Pilate therefore went outside to them and says, What accusation do you bring against this human?
They answered and said to him, If this one were not doing harm, we would not have handed him over to you.
Pilate therefore said to them, You take him and judge him according to your Law. The Judeans said to him, It is not permitted for us to kill anyone,
so that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke signifying by what kind of death he was about to die.
Pilate therefore entered the praetorium again, called Jesus, and said to him, Are you the king of the Judeans?
Jesus answered, Do you say this from yourself, or did others say it to you about me?
Pilate answered, Am I Judean? Your own nation and chief priests handed you over to me. What did you do?
Jesus answered, My reign is not from this world. If my reign were from this world, my servants would have struggled so that I might not be handed over. But now my reign is not from here.

Pilate therefore said to him, So you are a king? Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. For this I have been born, and for this I have come into the world: to bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is from the truth hears my voice.
Pilate says to him, What is truth? And saying this, he went out again to the Judeans and says to them, I find no cause in him.
But there is a custom for you that I release one to you at the Passover. Do you therefore wish that I release to you the king of the Judeans?
They cried out again, saying, Not this one, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber/insurrectionist.
